meta.marketNote: NSE/BSE closed for the weekend — Friday 19 June close shown
meta.trendLine: Today in markets: NSE/BSE are closed for the weekend, so Friday's close remains the latest session. The bigger story landed at Reliance's AGM: Jio's IPO has formally launched with its DRHP filed with SEBI, on track to become India's largest-ever public issue. Gold and silver rates in Chennai are flat and steady today.
meta.sodTitle: Reliance AGM: Jio IPO DRHP Filed With SEBI — Set to Become India's Largest-Ever Public Issue
meta.sodBody: Mukesh Ambani announced that Jio Platforms' board has cleared its IPO and the DRHP was filed with SEBI on Friday, expected to raise around $4 billion. Reliance also unveiled a Rs 1,10,000 crore sovereign AI infrastructure push in Jamnagar and posted record FY26 revenue, EBITDA, and net profit.

multibaggars.name: Ion Exchange (India) Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Only listed pure-play water treatment company in India. PLI-era industrial demand + Jal Jeevan Mission municipal contracts = two independent growth engines.
multibaggars.risk: Order execution risk; lumpy quarterly results

multibaggars.name: Nesco Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Owns Bombay Exhibition Centre (India's largest MICE venue) + IT park income. Massive land bank in Mumbai with near-zero debt. Post-COVID MICE recovery still underway.
multibaggars.risk: Event-driven revenue volatility; low trading liquidity

multibaggars.name: Clean Science & Technology Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Global leader in MEHQ and BHA — antioxidant chemicals with no domestic competitor. Export-driven with pricing power. India's specialty chemicals capex cycle adds tailwind.
multibaggars.keyMetric: ROCE >30% | Net cash positive | 5Y PAT CAGR ~22%
multibaggars.risk: Single-product concentration; Chinese competition risk on margins

multibaggars.name: Hindustan Zinc Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: World's second-largest zinc-lead miner. EV battery demand (zinc-air batteries) and galvanising demand from infrastructure spending = structural demand upgrade. Massive dividend yield.
multibaggars.keyMetric: Dividend yield ~6% | ROCE >35% | Net cash ₹14,000+ cr
multibaggars.risk: Commodity price cycles; government divestment overhang

multibaggars.name: Indraprastha Gas Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Delhi NCR's monopoly CNG distributor. EV risk is overstated — CNG cars still the most practical affordable option for Delhi's 3M+ auto-rickshaw and taxi fleet. PNG household penetration still only ~40%.
multibaggars.risk: APM gas price policy risk; EV adoption acceleration

stories.title: Reliance AGM: Jio IPO DRHP filed with SEBI — on track to be India's largest-ever public issue
stories.why: This is the most anticipated stock market debut in Indian history, and it just moved from intent to formal filing.
stories.summary: Mukesh Ambani chaired Reliance Industries' 49th AGM on Friday June 19, announcing that the Jio Platforms board has cleared its IPO and the DRHP was filed with SEBI the same day. The issue is expected to raise around $4 billion through a fresh issue of up to 27 crore shares, with Akash, Isha, and Anant Ambani leading the process. Jio Platforms reported Q4 net profit of Rs 7,935 crore, up 13% year-on-year. Ambani also unveiled a sovereign AI infrastructure push — a Rs 1,10,000 crore commitment including a 120 MW AI data center in Jamnagar powered by renewable energy. RIL posted record FY26 consolidated revenue of Rs 11.76 trillion, up 9.8% year-on-year.
stories.take: This single filing does double duty: it is both India's biggest IPO story in years and a serious bet that India can build sovereign AI infrastructure rather than rent it from US hyperscalers — worth tracking both threads independently as the listing process unfolds.

stories.title: Sensex, Nifty snap five-day winning streak as Accenture's guidance cut sparks an IT-led selloff
stories.why: India's benchmark indices fell for the first time in six sessions, with the damage concentrated almost entirely in IT stocks.
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points to close at 76,802.90 and Nifty 50 declined 154.90 points to 24,013.10 on Friday June 19. The selloff was triggered after Accenture forecast revenue below estimates and flagged a $400 million Middle East hit. The Nifty IT index crashed 5.43% to 26,919.85 — its steepest single-day fall in recent memory, touching a three-year low. India VIX spiked over 5%. Markets are closed for the weekend; this remains the most recent trading session.
stories.take: A classic example of imported risk — Indian IT exporters live and die by US/European client budgets, and a single guidance cut from a global peer can wipe out a week of gains.

stories.title: NTPC, Bharti Airtel and Eternal buck the trend — defence, power, telecom cushion the broader fall
stories.why: Not every sector fell — power, telecom, and defensive consumer names held firm, showing rotation rather than panic.
stories.summary: Despite the broad IT-led selloff, several Nifty 50 constituents posted gains. Eternal ended as the day's top performer, up 2.05% to Rs 263.85. Bharti Airtel rose 1.71% to Rs 1,906.90, Power Grid Corporation added 1.35% to Rs 292.60. Meanwhile, Infosys was the session's biggest loser, plunging 7.87% to Rs 1,038.80, followed by TCS at -5.90% and Tech Mahindra at -4.89%.
stories.take: The fact that domestic-demand names (telecom, power, FMCG, pharma) held up while export-dependent IT cratered tells you this was a sector-specific shock, not a broad risk-off event — a healthier pattern than an across-the-board crash.

stories.title: Gold holds firm in Chennai at Rs 13,600/g (22K) — flat over the weekend as Tamil Nadu demand stays resilient
stories.why: While IT stocks crashed, gold buyers in Chennai kept demand steady — a classic flight-to-safety pattern playing out locally.
stories.summary: Today's gold rate in Chennai stands at Rs 14,837 per gram for 24K and Rs 13,600 per gram for 22K, unchanged from Friday. 18K gold is priced at Rs 11,370/g. Demand in Chennai has remained on a steady upward trend through June 2026, driven primarily by jewellery purchases. The highest 22K price recorded in Chennai this month was Rs 14,630/g, while the lowest was Rs 13,500/g — today's rate sits closer to the lower end of June's range, a potentially attractive entry point for buyers.
stories.take: Gold's steadiness against equity volatility is a textbook hedge pattern. For Chennai households planning wedding-season or festival purchases, today's rate near the monthly low is worth noting.

stories.title: Silver in Chennai holds at Rs 255/g, unchanged over the weekend
stories.why: Silver is quietly outperforming as an investment hedge, with retail investors increasingly treating it as a gold alternative.
stories.summary: Silver in Chennai today is priced at Rs 255 per gram and Rs 2,55,000 per kilogram, per Goodreturns data — flat compared to Friday's close. International silver prices remain near $15 per ounce. Analysts note that recent excise duty increases on gold may be pushing some retail investors toward silver as a more accessible store of value, adding upward pressure on demand in price-sensitive Tamil Nadu markets.
stories.take: Silver's dual role as both an industrial metal and a more affordable precious-metal investment makes it worth watching alongside gold, especially for smaller investors who find 22K gold prices increasingly out of reach.

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire and a hawkish Fed created the tug-of-war behind this week's market swings
stories.why: Indian markets are caught between two opposing global forces — easing geopolitical risk and tightening US monetary policy expectations.
stories.summary: Investors spent the week weighing a US-Iran interim ceasefire (reducing crude oil price risk) against a hawkish tone from the US Federal Reserve, which kept rates unchanged but signaled a possible hike later this year. This created choppy two-sided trading — Thursday's gains driven by lower crude oil, followed by Friday's sharp reversal once the IT-specific Accenture shock hit.
stories.take: The ceasefire is good news for oil-import-dependent India, but a hawkish Fed complicates the rate-cut hopes that have been supporting risk assets globally. Expect continued volatility until one of these two forces clearly wins out.

stories.title: Broader markets diverge — midcaps and smallcaps hold up better than large-caps during the Friday selloff
stories.why: Smaller companies, less exposed to global IT spending cuts, weathered Friday's storm noticeably better than blue-chip names.
stories.summary: While the Nifty 50 and Sensex fell sharply, the broader market told a more resilient story. The Nifty Smallcap 100 edged up 0.25% to 18,753.05, the Nifty Smallcap 250 rose 0.33%, and the Nifty Microcap 250 advanced 0.46% — all gaining even as large-caps fell. This reflects how concentrated Friday's pain was in large IT exporters specifically.
stories.take: Domestic-focused smaller companies, which have less revenue exposure to global IT budget cuts, are proving more resilient right now than the blue-chip names most people watch on the evening news.

stories.title: Petrol at Rs 111.18/L, Diesel at Rs 97.83/L in Chennai — fuel prices steady as crude eases on the Iran ceasefire
stories.why: Fuel costs directly affect household budgets and transport costs across Tamil Nadu — and today's steadiness is good news after weeks of Middle East uncertainty.
stories.summary: Petrol in Chennai is priced at Rs 111.18 per litre and diesel at Rs 97.83 per litre today, holding largely steady. The stability comes as global crude oil prices have eased following the US-Iran interim ceasefire, reducing the geopolitical risk premium. LPG in Chennai stands at Rs 941.50 per cylinder.
stories.take: Lower crude prices are a quiet tailwind for India's import-heavy economy — every dollar drop in Brent crude meaningfully eases the current account deficit and gives the RBI more room on inflation management.
